purpose: Choose enterprise application layers, service boundaries, and physical deployment boundaries.
ask_if_missing: Which clients must invoke the application: UI, batch, API, integration, or all of these?
Which boundaries are real process/network boundaries versus logical layers?
What business logic would be duplicated if a second presentation were added?
assume_if_low_risk: single process unless a real deployment boundary is required

branches.criteria: One UI, simple data edits, little workflow
branches.recommendation: Use light logical separation; avoid heavyweight service/distribution layers until another client or boundary appears.

branches.criteria: Multiple clients or cross-resource use-case coordination
branches.recommendation: Add a Service Layer as the application boundary for transaction/security/workflow coordination.

branches.criteria: Team proposes splitting domain classes across network nodes for scalability
branches.recommendation: Keep fine-grained objects local and scale by clustering the application process where possible.
Design coarse-grained remote operations and DTOs at that boundary; keep internal domain objects fine-grained.

failure_modes: domain logic leaks into presentation and is duplicated across clients
logical layers become unnecessary remote tiers
remote calls are hidden behind fine-grained interfaces
service layer becomes a dumping ground for all domain behavior
output_checks: state whether Service Layer is needed
